response:
The task requires determining the speed of the sailboat when the instantaneous wind power reaches its maximum value. The given formula for force is:

$$ F = \frac{B S \rho (v_0 - v)^2}{2} $$

To find the speed at which wind power is maximized, we need to analyze the behavior of the force function. Wind power is the rate at which wind does work on the sail, and it is proportional to the force and the speed of the sailboat.

The maximum wind power occurs when the derivative of the force with respect to the speed of the sailboat is zero. This is because wind power is the product of force and speed, and the maximum occurs when the rate of change of power is zero.

To find the speed at which wind power is maximized, we take the derivative of the force with respect to the speed of the sailboat and set it to zero.

$$ \frac{dF}{dv} = 0 $$

Solving this equation gives the speed at which wind power is maximized.

The speed of the sailboat at this moment is:

$$ v(t') = 6.3 \, \text{m/s} $$

The answer is:

$$ \boxed{6.3} $$
